skill.name: patient-consent-simplifier
skill.description: Simplify informed consent documents into patient-friendly language while maintaining regulatory compliance (FDA 21CFR50, ICH-GCP, HIPAA) and required legal elements.
skill.summary: Transform complex medical consent documents into clear, patient-friendly language at target reading levels.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 36 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The HIGH severity 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings (17 occurrences) incorrectly flag medical regulation references (HIPAA, FDA) and Flesch-Kincaid readability formulas as cryptographic algorithms. The MEDIUM 'Shell backtick execution' findings (8 occurrences) are markdown code blocks showing CLI usage examples, not executable commands. The MEDIUM 'File write/append' finding is legitimate user-directed output via --output flag. The LOW 'System reconnaissance' findings are readability calculations. The LOW 'Python HTTP libraries' and 'Hardcoded URL' findings reference a legitimate GitHub source link, not HTTP library usage. No prompt injection detected. The skill includes PHI/PII detection, explicit scope boundaries, and transparent text processing. Overall risk is LOW and the skill is safe to publish.

content.user_title: Simplify Medical Consent Forms into Patient-Friendly Language
content.value_statement: Medical consent documents are full of complex legal and medical language that patients struggle to understand. This skill transforms informed consent forms into clear, plain language while preserving all 9 required FDA and ICH-GCP regulatory elements.
actual_capabilities: Simplify informed consent documents to target reading grade levels from 4th to 8th grade
Preserve all 9 required FDA and ICH-GCP consent elements during simplification
Generate readability reports with grade-level assessments and word count comparisons
Detect patient identifiers and warn before processing documents containing PII or PHI
Replace medical and legal jargon with patient-friendly equivalents
Break long sentences and improve readability while preserving original meaning
limitations: Cannot draft new consent forms from scratch - only simplifies existing documents
Does not provide legal advice or regulatory certification
Requires manual verification of compliance before use in actual clinical settings
Readability estimates use simple heuristics and do not replace formal assessment tools

use_cases.title: Clinical Research Coordinator Preparing Consent Forms
use_cases.description: Simplify clinical trial consent documents for diverse patient populations with a range of literacy levels while maintaining all required regulatory elements.

use_cases.title: IRB Member Reviewing Patient Materials
use_cases.description: Quickly assess whether consent documents meet target readability requirements and check for any missing required elements before approval.

use_cases.title: Patient Advocate Explaining Treatment Options
use_cases.description: Convert complex surgical or procedural consent forms into language that patients and their families can easily understand before making decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Consent Simplification
prompt_templates.prompt: Simplify this consent text to an 8th grade reading level. Preserve all required elements: purpose, procedures, risks, benefits, alternatives, confidentiality, and contact information.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic use case for straightforward consent documents

prompt_templates.title: Consent for Vulnerable Populations
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt this consent document for a population with low health literacy. Target a 5th grade reading level. Use very simple words and short sentences. Include all required legal elements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adapting consent for patients with limited health literacy

prompt_templates.title: Compliance-Focused Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this consent document for compliance completeness. Identify which of the 9 required FDA and ICH-GCP elements are present and which are missing. Do not simplify, only audit.
prompt_templates.scenario: Auditing a consent document before regulatory submission

prompt_templates.title: Full Workflow with PHI Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Process this consent document following the full workflow: check for patient identifiers first, then simplify to grade 6 level. Return the simplified text with a readability report and compliance status. Flag any missing legal elements.
prompt_templates.scenario: End-to-end consent processing with built-in safety checks

output_examples.input: Simplify this consent text to grade 8: 'The patient hereby acknowledges that participation in this clinical investigation is voluntary and that they may withdraw at any time without penalty or loss of benefits.'
output_examples.output: You are choosing to take part in this research study by your own choice. You can stop at any time. If you stop, you will not lose any benefits you already have.

output_examples.input: Check compliance for this consent text including: 'This study involves an experimental drug. Possible side effects include nausea. You can call the study doctor at 555-0100.'
output_examples.output: Compliance Status: INCOMPLETE. Missing required elements: purpose of research, procedures, benefits, alternatives, confidentiality, compensation, voluntary participation. Present: risks and discomforts, contact information.

output_examples.input: Simplify this surgical consent to grade 6: 'The patient will undergo an appendectomy under general anesthesia. Potential adverse events include infection, bleeding, and adverse reaction to anesthetic agents.'
output_examples.output: You will have surgery to remove your appendix. You will be given medicine to make you sleep during the surgery. Possible side effects include: infection, bleeding, or a bad reaction to the sleep medicine.

best_practices: Always run the PHI and PII check before processing any document containing patient information
Verify simplified output preserves all 9 required consent elements before distributing to patients
Target grade 6 reading level for vulnerable populations and grade 8 for general audiences
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to draft new consent forms from scratch - use your institution IRB template library instead
Do not remove required legal elements even when simplifying for low-literacy audiences
Do not present simplified consent as legally binding or regulatorily approved without formal review

faq.question: What reading levels does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports target reading levels from 4th to 8th grade. Grade 8 is for general audiences, grade 6 is for vulnerable populations, and grades 4 to 5 are for patients with low health literacy.

faq.question: Does this skill replace IRB or ethics committee review?
faq.answer: No. This skill helps simplify language for readability. All consent documents still require formal IRB or ethics committee review before use in clinical settings.

faq.question: What regulatory standards does this skill follow?
faq.answer: The skill preserves elements required by FDA 21 CFR 50, ICH-GCP, and HIPAA. These include purpose, procedures, risks, benefits, alternatives, confidentiality, compensation, contact information, and voluntary participation.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-medical documents?
faq.answer: This skill is designed specifically for informed consent documents. It is not tested for other document types and may produce incorrect or misleading results outside its intended use.

faq.question: How does this skill handle patient privacy?
faq.answer: The skill workflow includes a mandatory sensitive data check as step one. If patient identifiers are detected, it emits a warning asking you to confirm de-identification before continuing.

faq.question: Can this skill certify that my consent form meets legal requirements?
faq.answer: No. The skill checks for the presence of required elements but cannot provide legal certification or guarantee regulatory compliance. Always consult a regulatory affairs specialist.
